understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to solve each KA59 level within the step budget by maneuvering a pushable object (block) onto the goal tile(s) marked with a '+' in the semantic grid, triggering the engine’s WIN condition and progressing until the full set of levels was completed.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement operated on a 3-pixels-per-cell grid: MOVE_* attempted to shift the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) in that direction, sometimes pushing an adjacent pushable block if it was directly in front and the space beyond allowed the push. Walls/boundaries could block movement, and the movement_blocked flags were only hints (an adjacent object existed) rather than guarantees. Levels could have multiple selectable pieces; control was switched by CLICKing a selectable’s exact pixel/grid position as listed in the state (same coordinate system as player.position and objects), after which subsequent MOVE_* commands applied to the newly selected piece.
